meta.name: Buildings & Construction
meta.description: govOS free public service: a plain-language navigator to Maui County Code Title 16 (Buildings & Construction) — building/electrical/plumbing/energy permits, how the process works, what it costs and who pays, enforcement, disaster-recovery permits. Sourced to Municode + County DSA; hands off to MAPPS. First version, expanding.
meta.pageTitle: Title 16 — Buildings & Construction · govOS · Kilo Aupuni
meta.eyebrow: 12 Stones Global · Kilo Aupuni · govOS · public service

hero.lead: A plain-language navigator to Maui County Code Title 16 (Buildings & Construction): a live parcel lookup, how the building-permit process works, which permit you need, the codes that apply, enforcement, and the disaster-recovery track for Lāhainā. The County's official application, payment, and inspection system is MAPPS — this service explains the law and hands you off to MAPPS at the application step; it does not replace the County's record. This is a first version — sections marked expanding are being filled in from the ingestion corpus; nothing here is legal advice.

sections.title: ★ Parcel lookup
sections.note: Before you build, check the parcel. Enter a Maui County TMK (tax map key) to see its designations — county zoning, community plan, state land-use district, and especially whether it sits in a Special Management Area (SMA) or on Important Ag Land, both of which add review to a building permit. Pulled live from the Hawaiʻi Statewide GIS. For this parcel's actual permit and inspection history (not in GIS), the result links you to MAPPS.

source.html: Live designations: Hawaiʻi Statewide GIS — Parcels & Zoning. Permits/inspections via Maui County MAPPS (Development Services Administration). No parcel data is stored or fabricated — every field is fetched live.

sections.title: 1 · How a building permit works
sections.note: The general path a building permit takes under Title 16, administered by Public Works — Development Services Administration (DSA). Each step notes the expense and who bears it today. Applications are online-only through MAPPS.

steps.name: Create a MAPPS account
steps.what: You must have a MAPPS Customer Self-Service (CSS) account to apply online. Free to register.

steps.name: Prepare stamped plans
steps.what: Construction plans must be prepared and stamped by a design professional licensed in the State of Hawaiʻi (for work that requires it).
steps.pay: Expense: design fees · paid by: applicant

steps.name: Apply & upload in MAPPS
steps.what: Complete every step in MAPPS CSS, then upload the required documents and plans for your application (building, electrical, plumbing, grading, driveway, or demolition).
steps.pay: Expense: application + plan-review fees · paid by: applicant

steps.name: Plan review & agency routing
steps.what: Your submittal is routed to the Building Plans Review Section and any approving agencies — Planning, Fire, Water, and others depending on scope and location (SMA parcels get extra review).
steps.pay: Expense: County staff review · paid by: County (+ applicant fees)

steps.name: Permit issued → build → inspections
steps.what: Once approved, the permit issues. Request inspections in MAPPS CSS (My Work → Request Inspections) at each required stage.
steps.pay: Expense: inspection fees · paid by: applicant

steps.name: Certificate of Occupancy
steps.what: For applicable projects, a Certificate of Occupancy is issued after final approval — the building may then be lawfully occupied.
steps.pay: Expense: final approval · paid by: applicant

position.label: ⚖️ Position — James Langford (analysis, not law; a stance beside the rule)
position.text: Where the County imposes a requirement on a building applicant (an upgrade, a study, an off-site improvement as a condition of permit), the County should bear the cost of the requirement it imposes — not download it onto the homeowner or small builder.

source.html: County of Maui Building Permit & Development Services Administration (DSA); apply via MAPPS — DSA Building Permit ↗. Process summarized in plain language; exact requirements & fees are with DSA / in the cited chapters.

sections.title: 2 · Which permit do you need?
sections.note: DSA administers the County's building, electrical, plumbing, grading, driveway and related construction permits. Start each from MAPPS.

rows.v: New building, addition, alteration, repair, moving, demolition, change of use
Driveway / access onto a County road

source.html: Permit types from County of Maui DSA (administers building, electrical, plumbing, grading & related ordinances) + MAPPS Permits. Confirm the exact application type in MAPPS.

sections.title: 3 · Title 16 chapters — the codes that apply
sections.note: Maui adopts model codes (with County amendments) inside Title 16. Building-permit applications submitted on or after October 28, 2023 must comply with the editions below. Verify the exact section on Municode before relying on it.

rows.v: Rules for administrative procedures & civil fines (cross-ref; see §5)

source.html: Maui County Code Title 16 (Buildings & Construction), Municode · code editions & Oct 28 2023 effective date per County Building Plans Review Section. Title 16 spans §§16.04B.010–16.28.040.
source.expanding: full chapter list + per-chapter amendments from the ingestion corpus

sections.title: 4 · Lāhainā & disaster recovery
sections.note: After the August 2023 wildfire, the County stood up a dedicated disaster-recovery building-permit track in MAPPS for West Maui / Lāhainā rebuilds.

cards.title: 🌺 Disaster Recovery Building Permit
cards.body: The MAPPS track for rebuilding fire-affected structures — start here if you are rebuilding in Lāhainā / West Maui.

cards.title: Lāhainā Community Plan
cards.body: The community plan guiding West Maui / Lāhainā recovery (ties to the Title 19 service's Lāhainā module).

cards.title: DSA — Development Services
cards.body: The Public Works division that administers building, electrical, plumbing & grading permits.

source.html: County of Maui MAPPS — DSA Disaster Recovery Building Permit.

sections.title: 5 · Enforcement & civil fines
sections.note: Building violations under Title 16 are reachable through the same County enforcement engine as zoning. Not legal advice.
sections.html:
Title 19's enforcement chapter, §19.530.030, expressly reaches violations of Maui County Code titles 8, 12, 14, 16, 18, 19 and 20 plus related rules and permits. So a Title 16 building violation can be pursued by a director through a notice of violation and order that may require cease-and-desist, correction by a date, and/or administrative civil fines, with the order becoming final after 30 days unless appealed to the Board of Variances and Appeals (an appeal does not stay the order). Title 16's own Ch. 16.13 carries the administrative procedures & civil-fines framework specific to building matters.

The verbatim Ch. 16.13 civil-fine schedule (default amounts, escalation) is in scanned administrative rules that the ingestion session has flagged as OCR-blocked — expanding pending a text-extractable drop. Caps and mechanics: confirm at the source.

source.html: Maui County Code Ch. 19.530 (Enforcement) — §19.530.030 reach (sourced in the Title 19 service) + Title 16 Ch. 16.13 (Admin Procedures & Civil Fines).

sections.title: 6 · Who pays — the charter lens
sections.note: Labeled analysis — the same provision read through two charters. Not adopted law; an interpretive lens beside the Code.
sections.mauiCharter: Under the Maui County Charter, the Department of Public Works (DSA) is charged with administering the building code and issuing permits; permit and plan-review fees are set by ordinance / Council. The Charter frames building regulation as a delegated administrative duty funded substantially by applicant fees.
sections.sovereignCharter: Under the Sovereign Charter (12 Stones) lens, a permit is the County granting back a use of the resident's own property. Where the County conditions that grant on a requirement it imposes (an upgrade, an off-site improvement), the cost of that requirement should be County-borne — the resident should not be charged to satisfy the County's own condition.

position.label: ⚖️ Position — analysis, not law
position.text: Building fees that recover the cost of review are fair; imposed conditions (the County requiring something beyond the applicant's own project) are where the cost should shift to the County. Same rule, read two ways — so residents can see the choice.

sections.title: 7 · Rules → Code: candidates for codification
sections.intro: Provisions that currently live in the building administrative rules that, in our assessment, should be codified into Title 16 — so the binding standard is council-adopted and publicly noticed, not only in agency rules. The Code citations are verbatim-checked; the recommendation is a stance beside the rule.
items: Civil-fine schedule (Ch. 16.13). Title 16 routes building civil-fine mechanics to administrative rules. → Codify a single readable penalty schedule (default amounts, mitigation, escalation) so a builder can read their exposure from the Code itself. rule text OCR-blocked — expanding
Plan-review timelines. Review turnaround lives in practice, not the Code. → Codify a plan-review SLA so applicants have an enforceable expectation. expanding

Disaster-recovery expedite criteria. The Lāhainā recovery track's fee treatment and expedited-review rules are administrative. → Codify the recovery standard so it survives past any one administration. expanding

source.html: Analysis grounded in MCC Title 16 + Ch. 19.530; admin-rule contents expanding pending verbatim ingest.

sections.text: Free now (no account): this page, the live parcel lookup, the process navigator, and the permit/chapter tables are all Tier 1 — free. Coming — Tier 3 (verified tools): a saved permit-readiness checklist, document-package helpers, and an AI permit assistant for Title 16 will be verification-gated (identity-verified). Identity is verified through the payment processor — only verified-status is stored here, never the raw ID. No live billing is enabled (Stripe keys are environment references only).
